Ethan Park: Hi team! Quick onboarding question: I'd like to add my partner as a dependent for dental + vision. I accepted the offer 2024-05-02 and set up initial elections, but I don't see 'domestic partner' in the dropdown. What paperwork do I need and how does this affect payroll? My first paystub already went out last Friday.

Lina (People Ops): Welcome, Ethan! We do cover domestic partners. You'll need the domestic partner affidavit + a proof of shared address (recent mail, lease, or joint bank stmt). I'll DM the affidavit PDF. If you submit within 30 days we can request an effective date back to your start date.

Sam (Benefits): Thanks Lina. Adding details: send both docs to benefits@redwood.com. If you want retro coverage to 2024-05-02, include "RETRO" in the subject line. Carriers sometimes bill retro charges; we'll flag payroll to handle proration if approved.

Ethan Park: Cool. If retro is approved, will payroll take a lump sum deduction next paycheck or spread it out? Trying to plan budget.

Lina (People Ops): Tagging @PayrollBot for specifics. Also note: if your first pay already processed before benefits were added, we typically apply deductions starting the next pay, and payroll will attempt a catch-up proration for any retro premium period.

PayrollBot: PayrollBot: Standard behavior: deductions begin the pay period after benefits enrollment is processed. For retro effective dates, payroll computes a prorated deduction to recover premiums for the earlier period unless the carrier bills us directly. If there are carrier chargebacks, Benefits will coordinate reimbursements. Any large one-time amounts are split over the subsequent payrolls on request to reduce impact.

Sam (Benefits): One more thing 56 if you're enrolling in vision + dental only, carriers tend to be easier on retro than medical. But confirm you included partner affidavit + proof. Also, if your partner is on COBRA, enroll now and we'll help terminate COBRA to avoid double coverage.

Ethan Park: They are on COBRA until 2024-05-20. I'll upload docs now and note COBRA in the email subject. Should I wait until COBRA ends?

Lina (People Ops): No need to wait. Enroll now with your preferred effective date. Benefits will coordinate with the COBRA admin to stop billing once new coverage is active. Upload asap to be safe.

Ethan Park: Uploaded affidavit + utility bill to benefits@redwood.com with subject "RETRO + COBRA". Thanks!

Sam (Benefits): Got it, Ethan. We'll confirm with the carrier and reply here. If payroll needs to do a proration, we'll note that and Payroll will follow up if a catch-up deduction is required.

PayrollBot: Noted: watch for retro enrollment from Ethan Park. If catch-up required, payroll will attempt to split the deduction across the next 2 pay runs unless Ethan requests otherwise.

Ethan Park: Awesome 56 appreciate the fast help.

source.messages:
Ava Chen: Quick PSA — after the candidate view UI rollout this morning, interviewers report the 'Submit evaluation' link is missing for most roles. They can open the candidate card but the eval form link/button is hidden behind the new actions menu.
Raj Patel: Thanks Ava. Can you paste a quick repro (steps + example candidate id)?
Ava Chen: Steps:
1) Go to Candidates > Search "Frontend SDE"
2) Click a candidate (e.g., cid=48291)
3) Actions menu shows "Share" / "Add note" but no "Submit evaluation"
Direct link that used to work: https://ats.redwood-inference.com/eval/48291
Emma Liu: Seeing same for iOS interviews — tried incognito and colleague in EU. :confused:
Micah Rivera: I can take a look. Any recent front-end toggle? Possibly the role-permissions mapping.
deploy-bot: Deployment 2026-03-23T09:12Z to channel-ui completed successfully. :rocket:
Ava Chen: TPM said rollout was staged this morning. Raj — can you confirm if this is a permissions flag (we didn't change reviewer roles)?
Raj Patel: I checked the role mapping table: reviewer -> can_submit_eval = true. Looks correct.
Micah Rivera: I found the bug — the new actions menu filters items by "menuVisibility" which used the old role string "interviewer" but the backend now sends "reviewer". Quick fix in PR #3221 to accept both. Pushing now.
Micah Rivera: Pushed hotfix to staging, tag: hotfix/eval-link-accept-reviewer
deploy-bot: Deployment to production scheduled in 5m (canary enabled).
Emma Liu: any workaround in the meantime? Some interviewers need to submit post-interview.
Raj Patel: Workaround: append /eval/<cid> to the platform root e.g. https://ats.redwood-inference.com/eval/48291 — link still opens if they have perms.
Ava Chen: Confirmed workaround works for two interviewers. Can we get ETA on prod fix?
Micah Rivera: Canary is in. If no errors in 10m I'll roll full prod. Expect <30m.
deploy-bot: Canary completed — 0 errors. Rolling full prod now.
Ava Chen: Verified in prod — eval button visible again for reviewers. 
Emma Liu: Verified on EU accounts too. Thanks!! :tada:
Ava Chen: I'll send a quick note to Interviewer Slack and update training doc with workaround in case of rollbacks.
Raj Patel: Thanks team. Logged incident/INC-2026-031 about the mismatch and assigned to eng runtime to follow up on contract tests.
Micah Rivera: Added a unit test to cover role string variations. Will follow up with a small migration script to normalize role names.
Ava Chen: Great. Closing thread — will ping if any more reports. :white_check_mark:

Aisha Rahman: FYI recruiting booked Noah Rivera for onsite Tue. Arrival 8:30am, looped for 9:00 start. Needs: hotel comp, airport pickup, parking, dietary (halal), and daycare parking note. @marcus @riya @ben
Marcus Li: Hi! I'll handle airport pickup. Flight lands 7:05am. ETA to office around 8:15 depending on traffic. What terminal?
Aisha Rahman: Terminal 2. Driver will be at curb with "Redwood" sign. I'll DM the driver's number to Marcus.
Riya Desai: Hotel comp approved. Booked at Harbor Inn (walking distance) for Mon night. Confirmation #H-9921. I'll forward the reservation and put an envelope for incidentals at reception.
Ben Carter: Parking: reserving a visitor pass in Lot B, spot VB-03. Need plate to finalize. Also will add a sheltered drop-off note.
Noah Rivera: Plate for rental is 7MN-5XZ. Thanks everyone. Also I have a 6mo-old and might need a quiet room to pump — is there a lactation room nearby?
Aisha Rahman: Thanks for mentioning, Noah. Yes, we have a private lactation room on 3rd floor. I'll add it to your itinerary and let security know to escort you there if needed.
Onboarding Bot: Hotel booking sent to Noah Rivera: Harbor Inn H-9921, check-in 2026-04-06. Temporary badge created: VIS-80612, expires 2026-04-06 20:00.
Marcus Li: Driver confirmed. I'll meet them at Terminal 2 curb G; expect to leave airport by 7:20. Marcus will text when boarded.
Riya Desai: Dietary noted: halal + nut allergy. I'll tag the catering request. Anyone need printed interview packets?
Ben Carter: Yes please print one packet + a map to lactation room and the parking permit. I'll place them in the reception folder.
Aisha Rahman: Also travel stipend: Noah asked about taxi fare from airport to hotel. We covered shuttle but if he prefers taxi we'll pre-authorize up to $40. @noah confirm preference?
Noah Rivera: Shuttle is fine, thanks. For reimbursement I sent scans of a train receipt this morning.
Riya Desai: Received receipts. We'll include a pre-filled reimbursement form with the packet; Noah can sign on arrival.
Marcus Li: Quick note: interview panel has a 12:30 lunch break. If the schedule slips we can use the Zoom fallback link in the invite.
Ben Carter: Security will escort Noah from lobby at 8:25 to the reception desk, then to conference "Sequoia". I'll also leave a small welcome card.
Aisha Rahman: One last thing: could someone take a 3-shot headshot (for internal ATS) once he checks in? Low-res is fine. @riya or @ben?
Riya Desai: I can take it quickly.
Noah Rivera: Appreciate all the details. Looking forward to it. If anything changes with my flight I'll update here.
Aisha Rahman: Thanks team — everything noted. Ping here if any changes. :wave:

source.messages:
Ethan Park: Hi all 
 need to add a kid to my benefits. I just became legal guardian last week. Is that a QLE and what docs are required?

Nora (People Ops): Congrats Ethan! Yes, legal guardianship is typically a qualifying life event. We
 need the court guardianship order or an official placement letter that shows the date and your name as guardian.

Ethan Park: Awesome. The court order is a PDF but has case numbers I
 rather not expose fully. Can I redact some parts?

Lena (Benefits): You can redact sensitive info, but the document must still show the child
 name, DOB, the court signature/judge stamp, and the effective date. If carrier rejects for missing info we
 escalate. Use the secure upload link on the benefits Confluence page or send via encrypted email.

Ethan Park: Kid doesnt have SSN yet. We're applying. Will that block enrollment?

Nora (People Ops): Not usually. We can submit the QLE with SSN pending. Carriers often allow provisional enrollment and require SSN within 30-60 days. We'll mark the case as SSN pending and follow up.

Ethan Park: If I submit now, when would coverage start? Court order date was Sep 10.

Lena (Benefits): Most carriers set the effective date as the guardianship/placement date (Sep 10) if submitted within their QLE window (usually 30-60 days). Some carriers use the next of the month rule; we'll confirm on the case and tell you the expected payroll impact.

Ethan Park: Any impact on my HSA or dependent care FSA? Kid will need daycare sometimes.

Lena (Benefits): HSA: adding a dependent doesn't affect HSA eligibility unless you switch to a non-HDHP plan. Dependent Care FSA: foster/guardianship dependents living with you generally qualify for DCFSA reimbursement if they meet IRS criteria. Recommend confirming with your tax advisor for borderline cases.

Ethan Park: Do I need to update beneficiaries for life insurance when adding the child?

Nora (People Ops): Not required to enroll the child, but good practice to review beneficiaries. You can update life insurance beneficiary online in the benefits portal. If you want us to flag payroll for imputed income or beneficiary paperwork, say so in the email.

Ethan Park: Whats the subject line for submission?

Lena (Benefits): Use: "QLE - Add dependent (guardianship)". Attach court order (redacted if needed), proof of DOB (if not on order), and note SSN pending if applicable. We'll respond with a carrier case number.

Ethan Park: If carrier asks for additional docs, will you handle the follow-up?

Lena (Benefits): Yes 
 we handle carrier follow-ups. If they request additional proof (school record, immunization, placement letter), we'll let you know and help escalate to our broker if needed.

benefits-bot: Reminder: QLE docs should be submitted within carrier windows (commonly 30-60 days). Open enrollment Nov 1-15. Changes take effect Jan 1 unless carrier specifies otherwise.

Ethan Park: Thanks everyone  sending the court order now via the secure link. Appreciate it :thumbsup:

Lena (Benefits): You can redact sensitive info, but the document must still show the child
 name, DOB, the court signature/judge stamp, and the effective date. If carrier rejects for missing info we
 escalate. Use the secure upload link on the benefits Confluence page or send via encrypted email.

Ethan Park: Kid doesnt have SSN yet. We're applying. Will that block enrollment?

Nora (People Ops): Not usually. We can submit the QLE with SSN pending. Carriers often allow provisional enrollment and require SSN within 30-60 days. We'll mark the case as SSN pending and follow up.

Ethan Park: If I submit now, when would coverage start? Court order date was Sep 10.

Lena (Benefits): Most carriers set the effective date as the guardianship/placement date (Sep 10) if submitted within their QLE window (usually 30-60 days). Some carriers use the next of the month rule; we'll confirm on the case and tell you the expected payroll impact.

Ethan Park: Any impact on my HSA or dependent care FSA? Kid will need daycare sometimes.

Lena (Benefits): HSA: adding a dependent doesn't affect HSA eligibility unless you switch to a non-HDHP plan. Dependent Care FSA: foster/guardianship dependents living with you generally qualify for DCFSA reimbursement if they meet IRS criteria. Recommend confirming with your tax advisor for borderline cases.

Ethan Park: Do I need to update beneficiaries for life insurance when adding the child?

Nora (People Ops): Not required to enroll the child, but good practice to review beneficiaries. You can update life insurance beneficiary online in the benefits portal. If you want us to flag payroll for imputed income or beneficiary paperwork, say so in the email.

Ethan Park: Whats the subject line for submission?

Lena (Benefits): Use: "QLE - Add dependent (guardianship)". Attach court order (redacted if needed), proof of DOB (if not on order), and note SSN pending if applicable. We'll respond with a carrier case number.

Ethan Park: If carrier asks for additional docs, will you handle the follow-up?

Lena (Benefits): Yes 
 we handle carrier follow-ups. If they request additional proof (school record, immunization, placement letter), we'll let you know and help escalate to our broker if needed.

benefits-bot: Reminder: QLE docs should be submitted within carrier windows (commonly 30-60 days). Open enrollment Nov 1-15. Changes take effect Jan 1 unless carrier specifies otherwise.

Ethan Park: Thanks everyone  sending the court order now via the secure link. Appreciate it :thumbsup:

Riley Chen: Heads-up: final reminder going out in 48h for the review submission deadline. patching a small change to the export to include 'competency completeness' flag.
Harper Singh: nice. will that flag differentiate between 'ratings missing' vs 'narrative missing'?
Riley Chen: yes 2 it will be: COMPLETE, NARRATIVE_MISSING, RATINGS_MISSING, BOTH_MISSING. sample in the export looks good.
Ethan Park: schedule for last-minute extensions? we had a batch last cycle that came 2 days late and caused ripple.
Riley Chen: policy recap: auto-48h if manager checks extension box; any request beyond that needs people-ops approval.
People Ops Bot: automated reminder set 2 48h, 24h, 6h. final ping at 6am on due date.
Harper Singh: can we add a 'manager completion' checklist to the portal UI? a few managers missed confirming calibration prep.
Riley Chen: agreed. filing UX task: UX-3376 to add a completion checklist and confirmation modal.
Ethan Park: QA request: can people-ops run a final script to validate that all direct reports have a completed review record before comp export?
Riley Chen: yes. running validation script tonight and will post CSV of failures.
People Ops Bot: validation job finished. 27 failures exported to ticket HR-EXP-12.
Harper Singh: i'll handle outreach to managers for those 27.
Riley Chen: thanks. also note: remind managers to attach calibration notes if they want their rationale included in panel packet.
Ethan Park: do we have a retention policy for those notes? some managers worry about PII.
Riley Chen: retention: calibration notes stored for 2 years, access-controlled to people-ops and comp team. PII should be redacted per policy note in the guidance.
People Ops Bot: policy link: https://redwood.company/policies/review-retention (internal).
Harper Singh: thanks team. i'll run the manager reminder in the managers channel after the People Ops Bot sends the 48h ping.
Riley Chen: perfect. i'll post the exported CSV and a short outreach template.

source.messages:
Carlos Mendez: Hey team - accepted the offer, super excited. Quick immigration Q: I'm currently on OPT and my employer filed an H-1B. My school portal shows cap-gap extension, but I haven't received any receipt yet. Can I still start on the proposed Oct 2 start date? Also how does this affect benefits eligibility and I-9?

Sofia Martinez: Congrats Carlos! Were excited too. We can't give legal advice but we'll coordinate with our immigration counsel (Priya) to confirm. Operationally: we require a completed I-9 before payroll runs. Remote/authorized-rep I-9 is possible if you can't come onsite before your first paycheck. Please upload any passport, I-94, EAD (if any), SSN, and any H-1B receipt/notice to the secure HR portal and mark them private.

Ben Carter: Hiring manager here - team is OK with a remote-first start and flexible onboarding. If paperwork is delayed we can push new-hire training by a week. Keep us posted.

Derek Li: Payroll heads-up: cutoff for Oct payroll is Sep 25. If I-9 isn't complete before payroll processing we may not be able to pay on the first cycle; we will do our best to expedite but need docs in the system. Benefits enrollment window opens on your hire packet acceptance and must be completed within 30 days. Benefits effective date may be the 1st of the next month depending on plan rules. We'll flag your record once I-9 is done so benefits team can advise exact effective date.

Priya Rao: Hi Carlos quick note from counsel: if you have a cap-gap extension reflected in your SEVIS or your DSO has authorized cap-gap, that can extend your F-1 status/OPT work authorization under certain conditions. Please share any proof (DSO email, SEVIS stamp, or an I-797 receipt if you get it). We'll review and advise whether that will satisfy I-9 requirements for employment authorization. If you don't have proof in time, we can discuss start-date flexibility or a short contractor arrangement, but any contractor option requires legal and procurement signoff.

Carlos Mendez: Thanks. I uploaded passport.pdf, i94.png, opt_ead.jpg to the portal. No I-797 yet. My DSO emailed a cap-gap note - I put that in too. Is the DSO email OK for now?

Sofia Martinez: Got the uploads, thanks. We'll wait for Priya to confirm. For I-9, in many cases a receipt or DSO confirmation can be used temporarily while counsel reviews, but we'll log a follow-up requirement to present the final document once available. PeopleOpsBot: reminder - do not paste PII in this channel; use the secure portal for documents.

Priya Rao: I reviewed the DSO note you uploaded. It helps but we'll need either an official SEVIS cap-gap annotation or a USCIS receipt notice to finalize counsel advice. I'll email you privately with the exact docs we can accept for I-9 verification and any timing risks for travel or work authorization.

Derek Li: Payroll: once Sofia marks the I-9 complete well tag the employee record and run onboarding payroll checks. If the I-9 is pending we can set a manual payroll hold but that requires sign-off. Carlos, please confirm if you want us to hold pay until docs are final or try to process with a follow-up requirement.

Carlos Mendez: I'd prefer we try to process and Ill get any missing docs ASAP. If it becomes a problem, we can accept a one-pay-delay. Appreciate the flexibility.

Sofia Martinez: Noted. We'll attempt to process with a follow-up requirement. Priya: please DM next steps. Ben: I'll add a calendar note to postpone any instructor-led onboarding if paperwork delays beyond 1 week.

PeopleOpsBot: reminder_sent: Secure upload required for immigration documents. Public channels are not approved for PII.

Carlos Mendez: Thank you all - will watch for Priya's DM and update the portal when I get the receipt.

Ben Carter: Welcome aboard, Carlos. Catch you in orientation.

Sofia Martinez: Loop closure: documents received, counsel engaged, payroll aware, hiring manager flexible. Reopen thread if anything changes.

source.thread:
Amelia Tan: Quick update: small org restructure for Serving Control Plane and a new senior hire joining next week.

Maria Garcia: Hi team  we're splitting the Serving Control Plane into two narrower teams: Routing & Capacity (steady-state routing, autoscale, traffic policies) and Runtime Delivery (model rollout, runtime optimizations, quantization rollouts). This should help with ownership clarity and speed up rollouts.

Maria Garcia: Effective Mon 4/6. Samir Khosla will join as Senior Manager, Runtime Delivery. He starts 4/6 and will take over the manager slot for Runtime Delivery eng managers.

Jonah Reed: Congrats Samir! Welcome aboard. Can you share a short background?

Samir Khosla: Thanks! Formerly ran multi-tenant inference infra at Nimbus.ai, focused on rollout safety, KV cache improvements, and quantization-led perf wins. Happy to be here.

Maria Garcia: Reporting lines: Samir will report to Jonah for day-to-day, and partner with Priya on cross-team SLOs and canaries. No headcount change for Routing & Capacity lead.

Priya Natarajan: Welcome Samir! I'll schedule a runbook handoff and add you to the canary rotation doc. We'll also review the current canary list (prefix/cold-start/quantize) this week.

Samir Khosla: Appreciate it. Quick ask: please share active canary IDs and any known flaky tests. I want to avoid stepping on live canaries in first week.

Maria Garcia: Added links: canary rotation doc: https://redwood-internal/handbook/canary-rotations and hiring backlog: https://redwood-internal/recruiting/serving-reorg-plan. Samir has edit on both.

Amelia Tan: People ops note: onboarding checklist created and calendar invites sent. @People Ops Bot did the setup. Please accept 1:1 invites.

People Ops Bot: Onboarding checklist created for Samir Khosla. 1:1s, security training, and codebase access invites sent: https://redwood-internal/hr/onboarding/samir-khosla.

Jonah Reed: Staffing plan: the manager slot Samir fills was a backfill; we have two IC roles we will open next quarter (engineer for quantization pipelines, eng for rollout automation).

Maria Garcia: Action items for teams: update on-call rotations and escalation docs by end of week; we'll publish an updated org chart in the handbook after town-hall.

source.thread:
Maya Patel: Quick heads-up: we're consolidating Capacity + Runtime into a single team called Platform Performance reporting to Priya Natarajan. Effective 2025-04-14 Ethan Zhou will step in as Senior Eng Manager for the new team. This should improve ownership for batching, KV cache, autoscaling, and model routing. We'll keep the current oncall rotation for now. Two backfills approved: Staff SRE (runtime infra) and Sr SW Eng (batching/compilation). Leah will post reqs tomorrow. Please update team pages/org charts and join the AMA Thu 10:00. @Ethan @Leah

Ethan Zhou: Thanks Maya — excited to take this on. I'll schedule 1:1s with leads next week to sync priorities.

Rajiv Menon: Congrats! Quick Q: will Dedicated infra still route through Platform infra or be a separate reporting line? cc @Priya

Priya Natarajan: Dedicated remains logically owned by Platform Performance for routing decisions, but Dedicated infra billing/allocations still coordinate with Capacity PMs. We'll document the boundaries in the rollout checklist.

Kara Li: :tada: Congrats Ethan. For the Staff SRE req, do we already have an interview loop or should recruiting draft a starter loop?

Leah Kim: I'll post the req + hiring rubric by EOD tomorrow and spin up an interview-panel thread. Recruiting will handle initial screens. If folks have must-have interviewers, DM me.

people-ops-bot: Maya Patel added event "Platform Performance AMA" to calendar Thu 10:00-10:30 — attendees: all@company

Alex Gomez: Any immediate changes to oncall escalation? Our rotation might need extra headcount if we inherit more surface area.

Maya Patel: No immediate oncall changes. We'll reassess after two sprints and adjust if needed. If teams need interim coverage, DM me and we'll coordinate.

Ethan Zhou: Can someone grant me access to Dedicated capacity dashboards and SLO dashboards? @eng-infra

Samira Ahmed: I can add you. Ethan, send me the list of dashboards and I'll grant access today.

Leah Kim: Recruiting note: target hire timeline is 6-8 weeks from post. Flag any role that needs an expedited timeline now.

Priya Natarajan: One ask — team leads please update your team pages and oncall docs by EOD Friday so runbooks are current for handoff.

Maya Patel: Thanks everyone. Big change but positive for product velocity. Bring any discussion items to the AMA or ping me directly.

source.text:
Ava Chen: Hey team — quick policy update rolling out today regarding caregiver support and how it intersects with international travel. :wave: We heard from many folks who travel for work and need short-term backup care or travel-related caregiver reimbursements. Effective 2026-04-06 we’re introducing a caregiver support addendum to the travel & expenses policy. Full doc: https://intranet.redwood.com/policies/caregiver-travel (TL;DR below)

Marcus Diaz: thanks Ava. Is this under the existing travel budget or a new benefit pool?

Ava Chen: New line item — CARE-TRAV (a separate reimbursement bucket managed by People Ops and Finance). It won’t reduce your team's travel budget.

Luca Patel: FYI finance notes: approvals will use expense code CARE-TRAV. Receipts + short note required. We’ll route to People Ops for eligibility checks.

Joan Rivera: Key bullets for the rest of the company: 1) Up to $400 per qualifying travel event for short-term backup care (per-trip cap). 2) Up to $1,200/year per employee for caregiving-related travel expenses (for partner/child caregiving that must accompany you). 3) Emergency back-up care up to $150/day for up to 5 days in a row. 4) Applies to FT employees (excludes contractors) — see exception process below.

Riley Morgan: Quick Q — I’m an IC via agency, project spans 3 months and requires two week onsite visits. Any wiggle room?

Ava Chen: Short answer: standard contractors not eligible. Long answer: we have a discretionary exception process for long-term, full-time-equivalent engagements — ping People Ops (people-ops@redwood.com) and your manager. We’ll review case-by-case.

Samir (Legal): One note on international claims: receipts not in USD are fine, but you must include a bank statement or card transaction showing conversion, and a short justification that ties the expense to the business travel (e.g., "childcare while on customer trip in Berlin, 2026-04-10 to 2026-04-12"). Visa/immigration costs for dependents are not covered by this benefit unless pre-approved for business-critical circumstances.

Priya Nair: Implementation details: reimbursements use the new expense workflow in Concur. To submit choose: Expenses -> New -> CARE-TRAV. Attach invoice/receipt, short justification, and manager approval screenshot. People Ops will auto-approve routine claims within 3 business days; complex cases may take up to 10.

Luca Patel: Expense snippet we’ll accept (copy/paste in the notes field):

```
Expense code: CARE-TRAV
Reason: Backup childcare during customer trip
Dates: 2026-04-10 to 2026-04-12
Amount: 320 EUR (attach receipt)
```

Alex Müller: For EMEA colleagues: regional caps remain the same in EUR equivalent. Finance will publish a conversion table monthly. Also check local tax guidance — some countries treat reimbursements differently, contact Payroll before submission if unsure.

Joan Rivera: A few FAQ items we already put in the doc: "Can I combine this with PTO?" — yes, claimable regardless of PTO, but PTO is not required. "Is it retroactive?" — queries for travel after 2026-04-06 only; exceptions for urgent cases can be reviewed.

Riley Morgan: Thanks — appreciate the clarification on contractors.

Ava Chen: Quick note on privacy: submitted receipts go into Concur linked to your employee record; People Ops will redact sensitive PII when routing to Finance.

hr-bot: :memo: People Ops published "Caregiver Support & International Travel — Addendum" at https://intranet.redwood.com/policies/caregiver-travel. Effective date: 2026-04-06. For questions, contact people-ops@redwood.com.

Marcus Diaz: We’ll add CARE-TRAV to the monthly bill-run mapping and include a reconciliation column so managers don’t see their travel burn spike unexpectedly.

Priya Nair: If you want a quick 15-min walkthrough, we’re hosting two drop-in sessions next week (Mon 9am PT, Wed 4pm CEST). RSVP in the People Ops calendar event.

Ava Chen: Thanks everyone — will post a short digest and an FAQ to the #announcements channel later today. If you have specific edge cases, DM me or open a ticket at people-ops@redwood.com :heart:

source.text:
Amira Khan: Quick heads-up — we're standing up a small AI Trust & Safety org under Product to centralize policy, incident response, and customer escalations. Goal: consistent policy enforcement and faster cross-team response. No layoffs, just re-org.
Nora Elbaz: Recruiter note — I'll own the hire for "Head of AI Trust & Safety". Req goes live today. Target loop 4-6 weeks. Please DM referrals or drop them here.
Priya Natarajan: Nice. Who's the interim lead while we hire? And how do current policy owners get transitioned?
Samir Patel: I'll cover interim lead duties for ~8 weeks (policy routing, incident triage, escalation). Will keep my SRE slice minimal to avoid gaps.
Amira Khan: Thanks Samir. Short migration plan: current policy owners keep their reporting line for 30 days, then we'll move them into the new org in two waves. I'll post the timeline + org chart fragment here by EOD.
Lina Chavez: Recruiter ask — please post 3 outcome metrics and must-have vs nice-to-have for the role so I can tailor outreach.
Zoe Kim: I'll add the req to the hiring tracker and create a dedicated pipeline column "AI Trust". Also will schedule a quick sync for interviewers next week.
Priya Natarajan: Any immediate customer or SLA implications we need to flag to CS or Sales?
Amira Khan: No SLA changes. We'll notify CS only if we change account-level routing or if a customer-facing policy enforcement impacts a client. No external comms planned.
Nora Elbaz: Interview loop plan: 1) Recruiter screen, 2) Policy & ops case (take-home), 3) Cross-functional panel (Product, Infra, CS). Expect 3–5 week cadence if calendar aligns.
Samir Patel: I'll draft a 1-page incident triage runbook to hand off to the new hire. High-level flow: detection → triage → owner assign → mitigation → postmortem. Will add oncall contacts and escalation matrix.
Amira Khan: Quick FAQ to reduce DMs: 1) Compensation changes? No immediate changes. 2) Promotions/levels? Follow normal review cycles. 3) Concerns or questions? Reply here or DM People Ops.
Lina Chavez: Posting the JD in #jobs in ~20m. Recruiters will ping for referrals, please surface any internal candidates early.
Priya Natarajan: One request — please include a short list of expected cross-team collaborators (CS, Infra, Legal) in the job doc so interviewers can plan panels.
Nora Elbaz: Will do. Thanks everyone for the quick cover and help getting this off the ground. :sparkles:

People Ops: Heads up everyone  We're making a small org change in Platform: the SDKs & DevEx team is becoming its own sub-team under Product. Ava Lin will join as Manager, Developer Experience (new hire) starting Mon May 12. She'll own SDKs, quickstarts, and developer onboarding flows.

Jordan Kim: welcome Ava! she'll shadow the SDK roadmap for 4 weeks and then take ownership of the public SDKs + internal templates. we'll run a 30/60 plan together.

Ava Lin: thrilled to join  immediate focus: unblock onboarding friction for private customers, and tidy SDK release cadence. happy to do quick intro calls with teams next week.

Priya Natarajan: congrats Ava  i'll coordinate on the compliance bits that touch SDKs (telemetry, cert pinning). note: i'll be 50% on Partner Security this quarter so i'll hand off routine SDK approvals.

Tomas Berg: recruiting will open a senior SDK engineer req to backfill handoff work. target interviews in 3 weeks. i'll post the req + screening rubric today.

Samir Patel: engineering ops here  Samir and Ava will run two pairing sessions next week to walk through the CI/CD for releases and the package signing flow. i'll add the slots to #eng-platform.

Nora Elbaz: customer success note: we'll update the onboarding playbook and include SDK troubleshooting doc. Ava, can you add a short FAQ for common developer pitfalls?

Ava Lin: yes — will draft a 1-pager FAQ and sample app template by end of week. also planning weekly office hours for the first month (Wed 9-10 PT).

People Ops: action list: 1) People Ops to update headcount & org chart by Thu EOD. 2) Recruiting (Tomas) to open senior SDK req. 3) Samir to post pairing slots and CI checklist. 4) Ava to post 30/60/90 and FAQ. 5) Nora to stash updated onboarding playbook.

Jordan Kim: scheduling a 30-min cross-functional intro Tues 11am PT (Product/Eng/SE/CS). optional but recommended.

Tomas Berg: req live in Greenhouse now, screening rubric in the drive.

Priya Natarajan: sharing the compliance checklist in the shared drive momentarily.

People Ops: thanks team  congrats Ava & welcome aboard! reactions: :tada: :wave: :rocket:

Amira Khan: Quick update 1 — we're shifting Product ev liaison for Runtime into a dedicated Lead role to tighten cross-team rollouts. This is a reorg, not a headcount change.
Jordan Kim: I'll pick up the interim lead for the Product ev liaison starting Monday for ~5 weeks while we hire. I'll continue to own the Dedicated roadmap in a reduced capacity.
Priya Natarajan: Thanks Jordan. Who covers your Platform PM meetings while you're interiming?
Jordan Kim: Mariam will cover the weekly Platform standups and OKR syncs. I'll hand over the current action items by EOD Fri.
Lina Chavez: Req goes live today: "Lead, Runtime Product Liaison". Recruiting lead is Lina. Please DM referrals or drop them here. Target loop 33 weeks.
Evan Carter: Can we get 3 outcome bullets for the JD (30/60/90)? Helps with screening.
Amira Khan: Sure: 30d: stable cross-team rollout cadence; 60d: reduce rollout incidents by 25%; 90d: operationalize one standardized rollout playbook. Posting this to the shared drive.

Lina Chavez: Interview plan: quick screen, ops case study, cross-func panel. Please comment preferred interviewers.
Evan Carter: Add Product lead + Runtime eng mgr to the panel. Also would be good to include a CS partner for customer impact questions.
Amira Khan: FAQ: 1) comp changes? No immediate changes. 2) promotions? handled via normal cycles. 3) questions? Reply here or DM People Ops. Thanks team for fast cover.

People Ops: Quick update for the org 1 1 1 Ava Lin is joining Redwood Inference on Mon May 5 as Senior Solutions Engineer, Private Deployments. She9ll focus on onboarding, pre-sales technical scoping, and ramping our on-prem reference architecture. Welcome Ava! :sparkles:

Ava Lin: hey all! excited to be here. quick notes: i'll be pairing with Priya for the first month on compliance handoffs, and with Samir on the onprem runbook. open to 1:1s next week 1 drop a calendar invite.

Priya Natarajan: welcome Ava 1 i'll send the compliance handoff packet (SOC2/KMS bits) and schedule a review. FYI i'm shifting 20% of my time to Partner Security through Q3, so we'll run shared ownership until the new SE is fully onboarded.

Jordan Kim: congrats Ava! Product will set up a 30-min sync so you can see roadmap items that touch VPC and private. also pinging Sales for any active deals that need technical handoff.

Nora Elbaz: looping in CS 1 I'll align customer-facing docs and update the playbook with an "SE onboarding checklist". Team, please flag any must-know escrow or compliance customers.

Samir Patel: operational note: Samir (eng) here 1 Ava and I will run 2 shadow deploys next week for the onprem installer end-to-end. putting the pairing slots in #eng-oncall. also opening a tiny RFC to capture installer edge cases.

People Ops: hiring/backfill note: we're converting one existing contractor seat into a full-time SE req to cover post-ramp coverage. Recruiting will open the req today; target hire by end of June. Tomas will own the loop.

Ava Lin: thanks all 1 a small ask: can someone share the last two customer private deployments and any lingering action items? that'll help prioritize my ramp.

Priya Natarajan: will share de-identified summaries in the Drive and add a short "regulatory checklist" for each.

Jordan Kim: scheduled the Product intro for Tue 11am PT, optional for PMs/SEs/Sales. link posted in #product.

People Ops: short action list: 1) Recruiting to open SE req; 2) Priya to share compliance packet; 3) Samir to post shadow deploy slots; 4) Ava to run intro office hours in week 1; 5) Nora to update SE playbook. We'll post the org update to the company directory once the req is live.

Nora Elbaz: congrats Ava 1 excited to work together. reactions: :tada: :coffee:

Jordan Kim: :thumbsup:

Samir Patel: adding first shadow slot now.

Amira Khan: Quick org note: Jordan is moving from Platform PM to run Dedicated Capacity as interim Head of Dedicated, starting Mon. No headcount changes, just a leadership shift to accelerate Dedicated roadmap :rocket:
Jordan Kim: Excited to take this on. I'll be 60% on Dedicated, 40% ramping down Platform projects. Mariam will take over the Platform PM cadence while I transition.
Mariam Okoye: Thanks Jordan. I'll pick up weekly stakeholder syncs and the mid-sprint demos. Can someone add me to the Platform OKR doc?
Diego Morales: I'll update the ownership matrix and add Mariam to the delivery channels. Also adding Jordan to the Dedicated oncall shadow rotation for the next 2 weeks.
Nora Elbaz: Hiring note: we'll open a req today for "Capacity Lead (Dedicated)" to be a permanent hire. Recruiting lead is Nora; target loop 46 weeks. Please DM referrals.
Priya Natarajan: Any immediate customer impacts or SLA changes?
Amira Khan: No SLA changes. We expect normal delivery cadence. If we need capacity fallbacks we'll route to Hosted or alternate Dedicated pools and notify affected accounts.
Jordan Kim: I'll publish a short transition doc with the responsibilities I'm keeping vs handing to Mariam and the new hire. Also scheduling a 30-min sync Tue 11am for product/infra/sales enablement.
Mariam Okoye: I'll take care of the Platform backlog for the next sprint. Posting a handoff checklist and the updated stakeholder list in #product by EOD.
Nora Elbaz: Recruiter checklist: please comment must-have vs nice-to-have and suggested interviewers. I'll post the job in #jobs in 15m.
Amira Khan: FAQ: 1) comp changes? No immediate changes. 2) promotion/levels? handled through normal cycles. 3) concerns? reply here or DM People Ops. Thanks everyone for quick coverage. :wave:

Amira Khan: Heads-up: we're launching a small restructuring to combine Model Onboarding + Eval Ops into a new "Model Lifecycle" group under Runtime. No headcount changes, just clearer responsibilities.
Diego Morales: I'll run the team as interim lead for ~6 weeks while we hire a permanent manager. Will keep a slim infra ownership until handoff is complete.
Priya Natarajan: Congrats Diego 389. Who will cover your infra duties (deploy approvals / capacity) during the interim?
Diego Morales: Good Q. Raya will cover deploy approvals and capacity planning; I'll retain control-plane tasks like model version pins until we hand off.
Raya Olson: Confirmed. I'll update PagerDuty rotations and publish an interim oncall schedule + handoff checklist in #eng-infra by EOD Thur.
Lina Chavez: Req goes live today for "Senior Manager, Model Lifecycle" (IC+people manager). Recruiter lead is Lina. Please DM referrals or drop them here. I'll post to #jobs in ~30m.
Evan Carter: Can we get a short role doc (3 bullets: outcome metrics, must-have skills, interview bar) to speed outreach?
Lina Chavez: Yes please add those three bullets as replies below. Also drop any priority candidate sources.
Amira Khan: Quick FAQ to reduce noise: 1) Will comp change for current folks? No. 2) Any SLA risk? No immediate changes; we'll use Dedicated fallback routing if capacity dips. 3) How to raise concern? Reply here or DM People Ops.
Diego Morales: I'll publish a 1-pager runbook summarizing responsibilities I'm handing to Raya vs what stays with Runtime, plus escalation contacts. Also scheduling a 30-min sync Tue 10am with product/CS/infra.
Raya Olson: I'll add an incident triage checklist to the runbook and update the escalation matrix tonight.
Priya Natarajan: Thanks all. Can we get visibility into the interview loop (onsite vs remote, panel composition)?
Lina Chavez: Target loop = 3 stages: screen (recruiter+hiring mgr), technical + ops deep-dive, leadership+cross-func panel. Expect 3	6 weeks to hire.

source.text:
Noah Martinez: Hey @jamie — quick question. I need to request an ADA accommodation for recurring lower back pain. My doc recommended 2 fixed remote days/week and an ergonomic chair. Ava (my manager) knows and suggested I check with People Ops. What documentation do you need and what's the process?

Jamie Lee (People Ops): Thanks for flagging this, Noah. Sorry to hear you're dealing with that. We can absolutely help. At a high level: we need a short medical note (can be a one-page PDF) that states the functional limitation and recommended accommodations, then we route it through our accommodation review. We keep records private.

Noah Martinez: Appreciate it. Does the medical note need specific language? My doc can write something generic. Also does requesting something like remote days affect my probation/performance review?

Jamie Lee (People Ops): Short answer: no, requesting an accommodation should not be used against you in performance evaluations. We follow ADA principles + Redwood policy. For the note, generic is fine — e.g., "Employee requires intermittent remote work and ergonomic seating due to chronic back condition." If you prefer, you can redact diagnosis details; functional impact is enough.

Ava Chen (Manager): I support this request. I can confirm we've discussed and I can work with scheduling to make the 2 remote days fit the team calendar. Happy to provide a manager note if that helps.

Jamie Lee (People Ops): Helpful — thanks Ava. Noah, next steps: 1) DM me the medical note or upload via the accommodation form. 2) We'll review and respond within 3–5 business days with proposed accommodations. 3) We'll coordinate logistics (ship chair, update calendar policy, trial period). 4) If approved, we'll add a private note to your HR file and set a 30-day check-in.

HR Bot: Accommodation intake form submitted: https://redwood.company/hr/accommodations/form

Noah Martinez: Sending the note now. Quick logistics Q — does Redwood cover the cost of an ergonomic chair for home use?

Jamie Lee (People Ops): Yes, ergonomic equipment is commonly covered when recommended. We'll evaluate cost/ship options vs. stipend. If the chair exceeds the standard stipend, we may route for approval but that's mostly administrative.

Noah Martinez: Awesome, thanks. One more thing — timeline for confidential handling? I need to make sure my direct team only hears what's necessary.

Jamie Lee (People Ops): Confidentiality: only People Ops + your manager + benefits admin (if needed for procurement) will see the note. We will only share accommodation-related details with the team at the level needed (e.g., "Noah will be remote Tue/Thu, please sync async"). We'll not disclose medical specifics.

Ava Chen (Manager): Thanks — I'll keep it to the schedule change and logistics.

Noah Martinez: Great, thanks everyone. DMing the PDF now. :thumbsup:

Jamie Lee (People Ops): Got it. I'll confirm receipt and follow up with a draft accommodation plan by EOD Friday. If anything urgent comes up, please ping me.

HR Bot: Ticket #AC-20274 created for Noah Martinez (status: intake).

sara kim: Quick company update — short-term leadership rotation for Platform Ground. I'm stepping in as interim Platform Director for ~8 weeks starting Mon Apr 7 to cover Diego's parental leave. Will post calendar block later.

ajay patel: Thanks Sara — congrats Diego! quick Q: what are the top priorities I should own as runtime lead while you're covering director duties?

sara kim: Ajay — focus on the runtime P0s (KV cache regressions, nightly OOMs), keep the canary rollout for v2.3 paused until we have the perf traces cleared. I'll own cross-team escalations and external stakeholders.

ana torres: I'll take capacity & infra ops — planning to hold a 30m handoff with Diego + oncall tonight. Can someone drop the handoff checklist here? :white_check_mark:

people-ops-bot: Reminder: submit interim leadership coverage form — https://intranet.redwood/people-ops/interim-cover (required for payroll/approvals).

priya naranjan: tagging @people-ops — do we need a change to headcount or is this a temporary IC backfill? Also, what's the suggested band for the 4-6mo backfill?

people-ops-bot: People Ops note: short-term backfills can be engaged as contractors or temp FTEs. Please complete the intake form above; recruiting will advise banding based on scope.

david c.: I'll own day-to-day oncall routing and triage. Ajay — can you own the weekly runtime sync doc so I can escalate when needed?

ajay patel: Yep, I'll own the runtime weekly doc. Putting a stub in the docs repo now.

sara kim: Hand-off doc (work in progress): https://docs.redwood/platform-ground/handoffs/2026-interim-sara-v1 — contains list of active rolling upgrades, canary status, and current bugs. Ana — please add infra runbook links.

ana torres: Adding runbooks now. Also, proposing a short org chart update for the console so cross-functional PMs know who to contact.

priya naranjan: Cool. Once the intake form is in, I'll coordinate with recruiting and share a hiring timeline (target to have contractor in place within 3 weeks). FYI — approval usually ~5 business days once form is submitted.

sara kim: Thank you all. Plan: 1) submit interim coverage form today (people-ops) 2) recruiting opens backfill req tomorrow 3) 1:1 handoffs between Diego and each interim owner this week. I'll post weekly check-in cadence. Any blockers call them out here. :heart:

david c.: no blockers from infra. Thanks — we'll sync after the handoff call.

ajay patel: One last note — pausing non-critical infra changes from Apr 7–21 to reduce blast radius during the rotation. Will post the freeze window in #eng-alerts.

sara kim: Good call. I'll add that to the calendar invite and notify PMs.

ana torres: Thanks team — glad we have a clear plan. :party_parrot:

Sasha (People Ops): Thanks for joining today's all-hands — opening a short follow-up thread for travel/offsites/desk/hub Qs. I'll fold clarifications into the Workstyle FAQ this week. https://intranet.redwood/workstyle :wave:

Miguel Torres: For the July offsite, can I book a multi-city ticket (EU -> SF -> SEA) and expense it? Do I need pre-approval?

Sasha (People Ops): Multi-city is fine if it's the most cost-effective routing. Prefer Travel Portal; if you must book externally, submit receipts and a short note explaining savings. Tag expenses `offsite-travel`.

Rekha Singh: Are coworking passes covered when I'm in-region for >7 days on business?

Sasha (People Ops): Yes — hub credits cover coworking up to 12 days/month for business travel. Use expense code `hub-credit` and attach booking confirmation. For non-approved spaces, please get a quick pre-clear from people-ops.

Ben Carter: Desk booking question — can I reserve the same desk 1 day/week for the quarter?

Sasha (People Ops): Use Office Booking -> Recurring holds. Limit is 2 recurring holds per user. If you need a fixed desk for medical reasons, file an accommodation request and we'll prioritize it.

TravelBot: Notice: Per-diem and city-tier updates effective 2025-05-10. See policies page for tiers and caps.

Miguel Torres: Visa invitation letters — what info do you need from me?

Sasha (People Ops): Full name as on passport, passport number, DOB, travel dates, and city. DM the details and we'll email the official letter (typical turnaround 48 hrs).

Sasha (People Ops): Quick expense example to paste into the form:

```
Expense title: July-Offsite-MultiCity
Amount: 965.00
Category: Travel - Flight
Receipts: itinerary.pdf
Tag: offsite-july-2025
```

Sasha (People Ops): Thanks — keep questions short and we'll update the pinned FAQ here. For one-off exceptions email people-ops@redwood.com or DM me. :spiral_note_pad:

source.messages:
Maya (People Ops): Quick org update — Priya Natarajan will join Redwood Inference as Director, Runtime Reliability, reporting to Tom. Start date: May 5. This creates two immediate needs: (1) a backfill for a Senior Reliability Engineer (L4) and (2) an interim lead for the observability stack for ~6 weeks while we onboard. We want an internal-first hiring window; details below.

Tom (CTO): Huge yay — Priya's background on low-latency inference infra is exactly what we need. We'll allocate a small contract budget to cover the ramp. :tada:

Priya Natarajan: Hi everyone! Really excited to join. I'm remote (PST) and will be async-first during week 1. Happy to read existing runbooks and meet the runtime, sres, and devex teams.

Evan (Eng Manager - Runtime): Timeline for the senior SRE backfill: internal apps open through Apr 10, interviews Apr 17–24, final decision by May 1 so we have overlap with Priya. If you want to recommend folks, DM me or drop names here.

Lina (Talent): Internal referral form: <https://company.internal/hiring/runtime-relief> — please fill out by EOD Apr 9. I'll kick off the Greenhouse loop after the internal window closes.

Ben (Eng Lead, Observability): Quick question on reporting: will observability engineers move under Priya or remain matrixed? I want to lock down owner for the alerting playbooks.

Maya (People Ops): Short answer: observability engineers remain matrixed to Eng Platform, but Priya will have a dotted-line for SLOs, runbook ownership, and incident postmortems during the transition. Full org chart and a short decision tree are in the transition doc.

Priya Natarajan: I'll set up 30-min intro office hours during the week of May 6 — open slots for 1:1s and live walkthroughs of the infra. Please send me the top 3 pain points & the dashboards you rely on.

Anita (People Ops Bot): Anita created a calendar: "Priya intro — 1:1s" and invited runtime, sres, and devex teams.

Chris (SRE): We should prioritize the cache-eviction bug — it's contributing to intermittent p99 spikes on hosted inference. I can take interim ownership for observability and run the on-call for the next 3 weeks if that helps.

Evan (Eng Manager - Runtime): Thanks Chris — yes please. We'll pair you with Priya on onboarding and handoff sessions.

Maya (People Ops): Action summary:
- Lina: internal app link + referral form (due Apr 9)
- Evan: interview calendar + hiring rubric (internal then external)
- Priya: intro office hours week of May 6
- Chris: interim observability lead for next 3 weeks (confirmed)
- People Ops: transition doc + org chart attached in thread (see doc below)

If you have candidates to recommend or questions about ownership, @Maya and @Lina are the go-to. Thanks all — please react with :thumbsup: if you saw this.

Lina (Talent): Internal referral form: <https://company.internal/hiring/runtime-relief> — please fill out by EOD Apr 9. I'll kick off the Greenhouse loop after the internal window closes.

Ben (Eng Lead, Observability): Quick question on reporting: will observability engineers move under Priya or remain matrixed? I want to lock down owner for the alerting playbooks.

Maya (People Ops): Short answer: observability engineers remain matrixed to Eng Platform, but Priya will have a dotted-line for SLOs, runbook ownership, and incident postmortems during the transition. Full org chart and a short decision tree are in the transition doc.

Priya Natarajan: I'll set up 30-min intro office hours during the week of May 6 — open slots for 1:1s and live walkthroughs of the infra. Please send me the top 3 pain points & the dashboards you rely on.

Anita (People Ops Bot): Anita created a calendar: "Priya intro — 1:1s" and invited runtime, sres, and devex teams.

Chris (SRE): We should prioritize the cache-eviction bug — it's contributing to intermittent p99 spikes on hosted inference. I can take interim ownership for observability and run the on-call for the next 3 weeks if that helps.

Evan (Eng Manager - Runtime): Thanks Chris — yes please. We'll pair you with Priya on onboarding and handoff sessions.

- Lina: internal app link + referral form (due Apr 9)
- Evan: interview calendar + hiring rubric (internal then external)
- Priya: intro office hours week of May 6
- Chris: interim observability lead for next 3 weeks (confirmed)

If you have candidates to recommend or questions about ownership, @Maya and @Lina are the go-to. Thanks all — please react with :thumbsup: if you saw this.

source.messages:
Ava Chen: Hey people-ops :wave: Quick questions from my first week — I submitted the new hire packet yesterday. Can someone confirm when my health insurance / benefits enrollment will activate? Also, how do I get reimbursed for the monitor I bought this morning? And when should I expect my first paycheck? Thanks!

Jorge Rivera (People Ops): welcome aboard Ava! :tada: TL;DR: benefits enrollment link below, reimbursements via Spendesk, payroll is biweekly — details: https://intranet.redwood/benefits-enroll. A few bullets: 1) Benefits portal: complete the enrollment within 30 days of your start date to get company coverage. You should see an invite email from our benefits provider (BeneCorp) — check spam. 2) Coverage effective date is usually the first of the month following 30 days of employment (so there can be a short waiting period). 3) Reimbursements: use Spendesk (or Concur) and attach receipts. Small one-off purchases <$200: you can submit directly; >$200 please ask your manager for pre-approval. 4) Payroll: we run biweekly pay on Fridays; new hires are paid on the next scheduled pay date after payroll cutoffs/process.

Ava Chen: Thanks Jorge — do you have the direct link to the Spendesk form? Also what exact info do you need for payroll (I already filled the basic tax form in the new-hire packet).

Lina Patel (Payroll): Hi Ava — welcome! :slightly_smiling_face: Spendesk: https://spend.redwood/submit New hire payroll checklist: - direct deposit form (link above) - gov ID + SSN in Workday (already submitted?) - completed W-4/State tax forms in Workday If all that is in by the Tuesday before payday cutoff (11:59pm PT), deposit goes through for that Friday. If you missed that cutoff you'll get the following Friday. Example: if you started and finished forms by Tue 03/05 cutoff, your pay day would be Fri 03/15. We also process a retro/starter payment if necessary but that can delay one cycle.

Ava Chen: I did upload my SSN + W-4, but not the direct deposit yet. Can I upload bank info securely via Workday?

Maya Ahmed (People Ops): yes — Workday has secure banking fields. If you'd rather not use Workday, you can also email payroll@redwood with a photo of a voided check but Workday is preferred. I uploaded the benefits summary PDF to this thread: "Redwood-Health-Summary-2024.pdf" (file).

Sam Brooks (Eng manager): Welcome Ava! For monitors / peripherals: if you bought something you needed immediately, submit on Spendesk and tag me as approver (sam.brooks). For anything larger (> $500) ping me first next time. :+1:

Ava Chen: Awesome — submitting Spendesk now. One more question: are dental/vision separate enrollment windows or included in the main benefits form?

Jorge Rivera (People Ops): dental + vision are part of the same enrollment flow. You can mix plans (e.g., single medical + family dental) in the portal. If you want help picking options DM me and I can walk through plan differences.

Ava Chen: And about holidays/PTO: I saw the handbook but couldn't find whether Redwood observes floating holidays for new hires during first 30 days.

Lina Patel (Payroll): Redwood follows the published holiday calendar (link in handbook). Floating holidays accrue after hire and are pro-rated based on start date. PTO accruals begin from day 1 but some policies restrict usage until after 90 days — check your offer letter / handbook for the 90-day waiting rule. If you need a day off in the first month, ask your manager — we handle on a case-by-case for new hires.

Ava Chen: Great, that makes sense. One last thing — medical claims for appointments that happened before coverage effective date: can I submit and get retroactive coverage?

Jorge Rivera (People Ops): generally claims are only reimbursed for dates of service on or after your coverage effective date. There are a few exceptions (emergency/urgent care) but those require case review with BeneCorp. If you have a specific claim, DM me the date and provider and I'll start a case.

payroll-bot: Lina submitted direct_deposit_form for Ava Chen at 2024-03-07 14:23 UTC.

Ava Chen: Thanks everyone — I'll finish the direct deposit today and submit the receipt for the monitor. Appreciate the warm welcome! :blush:

Maya Ahmed (People Ops): Welcome again, Ava. Ping this thread if any of the links don't work. I can also set up a 15m one-on-one to go through benefits if you'd like.

Sam Brooks: FYI new-hires — if you need help with receipts or Spendesk tagging, ping eng-ops.

Ava Chen: booking the 1:1 with Maya now. Thanks all! :wave:
